research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — worker workRelationships child collection
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all worker work relationships
official_sources.used_for: exact child collection path under /workers/{workersUniqID}; q queryable attributes (WorkerType, StartDate, PrimaryFlag, LegalEmployerName, PeriodOfServiceId, TerminationDate); documented finders PrimaryKey and findByPeriodOfServiceId with PeriodOfServiceId variable; fields/onlyData/orderBy/limit/offset/totalResults options; items/count/hasMore/limit/offset/totalResults envelope; item links arrays with rel/href; PeriodOfServiceId as the row identity

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all workers
official_sources.used_for: framework query-parameter formats shared by all collections: finder=<name>;<var>=<value>[,<var>=<value>], q=expression1;expression2 as ANDed where-clauses, orderBy=field:asc|desc, totalResults as an ESTIMATED row count, offset default 0, hasMore as the more-rows indicator

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; version-4-and-above structured error document application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Status Codes
official_sources.used_for: documented 5xx server-error class used by the failing-page scenario

research.language_rationale: The HR reporting pipeline this walker feeds is a Node/TypeScript service; Oracle publishes no official JavaScript SDK for Fusion HCM REST and documents the surface as plain REST, so the built-in fetch and node:fs (for the durable checkpoint) are the credible integration path with zero dependencies, keeping the seed hermetic.

verified_facts: workRelationships is a child collection at /workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships with the standard collection envelope items/count/hasMore/limit/offset (+totalResults when requested).

finder syntax is finder=<name>;<var>=<value>[,<var>=<value>] (documented finders here: PrimaryKey, findByPeriodOfServiceId); q is a where-clause parameter whose ';'-separated expressions AND together; they are distinct query parameters.
The response's limit is the paging size the server actually used — it may be smaller than requested, so the next offset must be previous offset + returned count.
hasMore is the only loop condition; totalResults is an estimate and offset paging gives no stable-total guarantee, so walkers must deduplicate by row identity (PeriodOfServiceId) across pages.
Items carry links arrays ({rel, href, name, kind}) with absolute hrefs, including rel=self and rel=canonical entries.
With framework version 4+, failures answer application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), and ordered o:errorDetails entries.
